title: manisuris rugosa
intro: Manisuris rugosa, also known as sea buckthorn, is a thorny shrub native to Europe and Asia, recognized for its yellow-orange berries and bright green leaves. The plant has been utilized for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its high content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as flavonoids and fatty acids, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Manisuris rugosa include its ability to aid in wound healing, improve skin conditions, and boost the immune system, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Manisuris rugosa is often prepared as a juice, oil, or tea, and can also be used topically in the form of creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
